rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Assigning Sums Due Or That Will Be Due Judgment Debtor From Rental Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2024 line 14. ASSIGNEE PERRYVILLE RECOVERY CORPORATION ( C/O BREWER & BREWER LLP ) Notice Of Motion For Order Assigning Sums Due Or That Will Be Due Judgment Debtor From Rental Of Real Property Status report re: Notice of Stay of Proceedings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM DRILLING COMPANY, INC. VS. SWINERTON WEBCOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT THE HERRICK CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION [FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE CGC-21-591319] MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is DENIED without prejudice. The request is premature. A final judgment cannot be entered in this consolidated action as not all the parties are covered by the judgment. rulings.Case Title: FREDA A. LEEPER VS. PORTIA L. OSBORNE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF FREDA LEEPER , TRUSTEE OF THE FREDA A. LEEPER TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 27, 2016 Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee is continued to December 20, 2024. No later than December 6, 2024 each party to submit (1) a declaration listing no more than 3 proposed appraisers and the cost of a court ordered appraisal by each proposed appraiser; and (2) any evidence each party wishes the Court to consider in determination of the fair market value of the property in lieu of the court ordered appraisal should the Court determine that the evidentiary value of an appraisal is outweighed by the cost of the appraisal. By no later than December 16, 2024 each party to submit a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages), stating (1) whether they agree that Court ordered appraisal shall be ordered based on the evidence regarding its costs or the party wishes the Court to determine the fair market value without an appraisal based on other evidence presented by all parties; and (2) state any objections to the appraisers proposed by any other parties. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. FAY SERVICING LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: TRO AND OSC, RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2024 line 8. TRO AND OSC, RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet its burden for preliminary injunction. The Court Notes that the moving party failed to comply with the Court's October 30, 2024 order and there is no proof of service on file as of November 8, 2024. However, since Defendant filed an opposition on the merits, the request for preliminary injunction is considered and is denied on the merits. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
